speaker: DEBATE
text: We're live from Georgia, a key battleground state in the race for the White House in just

speaker: DEBATE
text: moments the current U.S. president will debate the former U.S. president as their party's

speaker: DEBATE
text: presumptive nominees of first in American history.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We want to welcome our viewers in the United States and around the world to our studios

speaker: DEBATE
text: in Atlanta.

speaker: DEBATE
text: This is the CNN presidential debate.

speaker: DEBATE
text: This debate is being produced by CNN and is coming to you live on CNN, CNN International,

speaker: DEBATE
text: CNN.com, CNN Max and CNN Español.

speaker: DEBATE
text: This is a pivotal moment between President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump

speaker: DEBATE
text: in their rematch for the nation's highest office.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Each will make his case to the American people with just over four months until election

speaker: DEBATE
text: day.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Good evening, I'm Dana Bash, anchor of CNN's inside politics and co-anchor of State of the

speaker: DEBATE
text: Union.

speaker: DEBATE
text: I'm Jake Tapper, anchor of CNN's the lead and co-anchor of State of the Union.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Dana and I will co-moderate this evening.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Our job is to facilitate a debate between the two candidates tonight.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Before we introduce them, we want to share the rules of the debate with the audience at

speaker: DEBATE
text: home.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Former President Trump will be on the left side of the screen.

speaker: DEBATE
text: President Biden will be appearing on the right, a coin toss, determined their positions.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Each candidate will have two minutes to answer a question and one minute each for responses

speaker: DEBATE
text: and rebuttals.

speaker: DEBATE
text: An additional minute for follow-up clarification or response is at the moderator's discretion.

speaker: DEBATE
text: When it's time for a candidate to speak, his microphone will be turned on and his opponent's

speaker: DEBATE
text: microphone will be turned off.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Should a candidate interrupt when his microphone is muted, he will be difficult to understand

speaker: DEBATE
text: for viewers at home.

speaker: DEBATE
text: At the end of the debate, each candidate will get two minutes for closing statements.

speaker: DEBATE
text: There is no studio audience tonight.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Free-written notes, props or contact with campaign staff are not permitted during the

speaker: DEBATE
text: debate.

speaker: DEBATE
text: By accepting our invitation to debate both candidates and their campaigns agreed to accept these rules.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Now, please welcome the 46th President of the United States, Joe Biden, and please welcome

speaker: DEBATE
text: the 45th President of the United States, Donald Trump.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Gentlemen, thanks so much for being here.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Let's begin the debate and let's start with the issue that voters consistently say, is

speaker: DEBATE
text: their top concern, the economy.

speaker: DEBATE
text: President Biden, inflation has slowed, but prices remain high.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Since you took office, the price of essentials has increased.

speaker: DEBATE
text: For example, a basket of groceries that cost $100 then now costs more than $120.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And typical home prices have jumped more than 30%.

speaker: DEBATE
text: What do you say to voters who feel they are worse off under your presidency than they were

speaker: DEBATE
text: under President Trump?

speaker: DEBATE
text: We got to take a look at what I was left when I became president and what Mr. Trump left

speaker: DEBATE
text: me.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We had an economy that was in free fall.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The pandemic was so badly handled.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Many people were dying.

speaker: DEBATE
text: All he said was, it's not that serious, just injected a bleach into your arm, it could

speaker: DEBATE
text: be all right.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The economy collapsed.

speaker: DEBATE
text: There were no jobs.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Unemployment rate rose to 15%.

speaker: DEBATE
text: It was terrible.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And so what we had to do was try to put things back together again.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And that's exactly what we began to do.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We created 15,000 new jobs.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And we brought out a position where we have 800,000 new manufacturing jobs, but there's

speaker: DEBATE
text: more to be done.

speaker: DEBATE
text: There's more to be done.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Working-class people are still in trouble.

speaker: DEBATE
text: I come from Scranton, Pennsylvania.

speaker: DEBATE
text: I come up household.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We're at a kitchen table of the things we're unable to be met during the month.

speaker: DEBATE
text: It was a problem.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The price of eggs, the price of gas, the price of housing, the price of a whole range of

speaker: DEBATE
text: things.

speaker: DEBATE
text: That's why I'm working so hard to make sure I deal with those problems.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And we're going to make sure that we have reduced the price of housing.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We're going to make sure we built two million new units.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We're going to make sure we cap rents so corporate greed can't take over.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The combination that I was left with in corporate greed is the reason why we're doing this problem

speaker: DEBATE
text: right now.

speaker: DEBATE
text: In addition to that, we're in a situation where if you take a look at all that was done in

speaker: DEBATE
text: his administration, he didn't do much at all.

speaker: DEBATE
text: By the time he left, there were things that were in chaos, literally chaos.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And so we put things back together.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We created, I said, those jobs, we make sure we had a situation where we now brought down

speaker: DEBATE
text: the price of prescription drugs, which is a major issue for many people.

speaker: DEBATE
text: If it's a $15 for an insulin shot, that's supposed to $400, no senior has to pay more

speaker: DEBATE
text: than $200 for any drug, all the drugs they can include beginning next year.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And the situation is making, we're going to make that available to everybody, to all Americans.

speaker: DEBATE
text: So we're working to bring down the price of it from the kitchen table, and that's what we're

speaker: DEBATE
text: going to get done.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Thank you.

speaker: DEBATE
text: President Trump?

speaker: DEBATE
text: We are the greatest economy in the history of our country.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We have never done so well.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Everybody was amazed by it.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Other countries were copying us.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We got hit with COVID.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And when we did, we spent the money necessary.

speaker: DEBATE
text: So we wouldn't end up in a great depression, the likes of which we had in 1929.

speaker: DEBATE
text: By the time we finished, so we did a great job.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We got a lot of credit for the economy, a lot of credit for the military and no wars and

speaker: DEBATE
text: so many other things.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Everything was rocking good.

speaker: DEBATE
text: But the thing we never got the credit for, and we should have, is getting us out of

speaker: DEBATE
text: that COVID mess.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He created mandates that was a disaster for our country.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Other than that, we had given them back a country where the stock market actually was

speaker: DEBATE
text: higher than pre-COVID, and nobody thought that was even possible.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The only jobs he created are for illegal immigrants and bounce back jobs, a bounce back

speaker: DEBATE
text: from the COVID.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He has not done a good job.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He's done a poor job in inflation's killing our country.

speaker: DEBATE
text: It is absolutely killing us.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Thank you.

speaker: DEBATE
text: President Biden?

speaker: DEBATE
text: Well, look, the greatest economy in the world, he's the only one who thinks that,

speaker: DEBATE
text: I think.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He's the greatest, the greatest economy in the world.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And the fact of the matter is that we prun ourselves in a situation where his economy,

speaker: DEBATE
text: he rewarded the wealthy.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He had the largest tax cut in American history, $2 trillion.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He raised a deficit larger than he president has in any one term.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He's the only president other than Herbert Hoover, who's had lost more jobs than he

speaker: DEBATE
text: had when he began, since Herbert Hoover, the idea that he did something that was significant

speaker: DEBATE
text: and the military.

speaker: DEBATE
text: You know, when he was president, there were still killing people in Afghanistan.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He didn't do anything about that.

speaker: DEBATE
text: When he was president, we were still finding ourselves in a position where you had a notion

speaker: DEBATE
text: that we were this safe country.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Truth is, I'm the only president this century that doesn't have any, this decade, that

speaker: DEBATE
text: no, any troops dying anywhere in the world like he did.

speaker: DEBATE
text: President Trump, I want to follow up if I can.

speaker: DEBATE
text: You won't have to respond to him.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Well, I'm going to ask you a follow up.

speaker: DEBATE
text: You can do whatever you want with the minute that we give you.

speaker: DEBATE
text: I want to follow up.

speaker: DEBATE
text: You want to impose a 10% tariff on all goods coming into the U.S.?

speaker: DEBATE
text: How will you ensure that that doesn't drive prices even higher?

speaker: DEBATE
text: I'm not going to drive them higher.

speaker: DEBATE
text: It's just going to cause countries that have been ripping us off for years like China and

speaker: DEBATE
text: many others in all fairness to China.

speaker: DEBATE
text: It's going to just force them to pay us a lot of money, reduce our deficit tremendously

speaker: DEBATE
text: and give us a lot of power for other things.

speaker: DEBATE
text: But he made a statement.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The only thing he was right about is I gave you the largest tax cut in history.

speaker: DEBATE
text: I also gave you the largest regulation cut in history.

speaker: DEBATE
text: That's why we had all the jobs.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And the jobs went down and then they bounced back and he's taking credit for bounced back

speaker: DEBATE
text: jobs.

speaker: DEBATE
text: You can't do that.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He also said he inherited 9% inflation.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Now, he inherited almost no inflation and it stayed that way for 14 months.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And then it blew up under his leadership because they spent money like a bunch of people that

speaker: DEBATE
text: didn't know what they were doing.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And they don't know what they were doing.

speaker: DEBATE
text: It was probably the worst administration in history.

speaker: DEBATE
text: There's never been.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And as far as Afghanistan is concerned, I was getting out of Afghanistan, but we were

speaker: DEBATE
text: getting out with dignity, with strength, with power.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He got out.

speaker: DEBATE
text: It was the most embarrassing day in the history of our country's life.

speaker: DEBATE
text: President Trump, over the last eight years, under both of your administrations, the national

speaker: DEBATE
text: debt soared to record highs.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And according to a new nonpartisan analysis, President Trump, your administration approved

speaker: DEBATE
text: $8.4 trillion in new debt, while so far, President Biden, you've approved $4.3 trillion

speaker: DEBATE
text: in new debt.

speaker: DEBATE
text: So former President Trump, many of the tax cuts that you signed into law are set to expire

speaker: DEBATE
text: next year.

speaker: DEBATE
text: You want to extend them and go even further, you say, with the U.S. facing trillion dollar

speaker: DEBATE
text: deficits and record debt, why should top earners and corporations pay even less in taxes than

speaker: DEBATE
text: they do now?

speaker: DEBATE
text: Because the tax cuts spurred the greatest economy that we've ever seen just prior to COVID and

speaker: DEBATE
text: even after COVID.

speaker: DEBATE
text: It was so strong that we were able to get through COVID much better than just about any other

speaker: DEBATE
text: country.

speaker: DEBATE
text: But we spurred that tax spurred.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Now, when we cut the taxes, as an example, the corporate tax was cut down to 21 percent

speaker: DEBATE
text: from 39 percent, plus beyond that.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We took in more revenue with much less tax, and companies were bringing back trillions

speaker: DEBATE
text: of dollars back into our country.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The country was going like never before, and we were ready to start paying down debt.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We're ready to start using the liquid gold right under our feet, the oil and gas right

speaker: DEBATE
text: under our feet.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We were going to have something that nobody else has had.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We got hit with COVID.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We did a lot to fix it.

speaker: DEBATE
text: I gave him an unbelievable situation with all of the therapeutics and all of the things

speaker: DEBATE
text: that we came up with.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We gave him something great.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Remember, more people died under his administration, even though we had largely fixed it.

speaker: DEBATE
text: More people died under his administration than our administration, and we were right

speaker: DEBATE
text: in the middle of it, something which a lot of people don't like to talk about.

speaker: DEBATE
text: But he had far more people dying in his administration.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He did the mandate, which is a disaster, mandating it.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The vaccine went out.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He did a mandate on the vaccine, which is the thing that people most objected to about

speaker: DEBATE
text: the vaccine.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And he did a very poor job, just a very poor job.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And I will tell you, not only poor there, but throughout the entire world we're no longer

speaker: DEBATE
text: respected as a country.

speaker: DEBATE
text: They don't respect our leadership.

speaker: DEBATE
text: They don't respect the United States anymore.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We're like a third world nation between weaponization of his election, trying to go after his political

speaker: DEBATE
text: opponent.

speaker: DEBATE
text: All of the things he's done, we've become like a third world nation, and it's a shame.

speaker: DEBATE
text: The damage he's done to our country, and I'd love to ask him, and will, why he allowed

speaker: DEBATE
text: millions of people to come in here from prisons, jails, and mental institutions to come into

speaker: DEBATE
text: our country and destroy our country.

speaker: DEBATE
text: President Trump, we will get to immigration later in this block.

speaker: DEBATE
text: President Biden, I want to give you an opportunity to respond to this question about the national

speaker: DEBATE
text: debt.

speaker: DEBATE
text: He had the largest national debt of any president in a four-year period, number one.

speaker: DEBATE
text: Number two, he got two trillion-dollar tax card benefited the very wealthy.

speaker: DEBATE
text: When I'm going to do a fix the taxes, for example, we have 1,000 trillionaires in America,

speaker: DEBATE
text: I mean, billionaires in America.

speaker: DEBATE
text: And what's happening?

speaker: DEBATE
text: They're in a situation where they, in fact, pay 8.2% in taxes.

speaker: DEBATE
text: If they just paid 24%, 25%, either one of those numbers, they've raised $500 million,

speaker: DEBATE
text: billion, I should say, in a 10-year period.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We'd be able to right wipe out his debt.

speaker: DEBATE
text: We'd be able to help make sure that all those things we need to do, child care, elder care,

speaker: DEBATE
text: making sure that we continue to strengthen our health care system, making sure that we're

speaker: DEBATE
text: able to make every single solitary person eligible for what I've been able to do with

speaker: DEBATE
text: the COVID.

speaker: DEBATE
text: I'm just going to be dealing with everything we have to do with...
